mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically compressing valuation multiples and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5 percent within a three-month window, it signals deteriorating labor demand that reduces consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.5pp over 3 months or exceeds 4.5%
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and consumer price index indicate a supply-driven disinflationary shock that compresses corporate profit margins and reduces real GDP growth expectations, dragging down equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint decline in INDPRO and CPIAUCSL does not predict negative 20d returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) across out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: In periods of elevated housing construction, rising private sector credit availability amplifies household purchasing power and consumption demand, driving positive equity returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: Positive correlation between HOUST and RSAFS and SPX forward returns breaks down during credit tightening episodes or when mortgage rates exceed 7%.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust labor demand that supports wage growth and aggregate consumption, which positively drives S&P 500 earnings expectations over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to predict positive 5d returns in > 55% of observations, or the signal's predictive power vanishes during recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 310, it signals entrenched inflation that erodes real disposable income and forces aggressive monetary tightening, suppressing equity valuations over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly negative when CPIAUCSL exceeds the threshold, or the signal generates false positives in > 40% of triggered windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling real GDP signals inventory accumulation driven by weak demand, forecasting a near-term earnings recession and negative equity returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The INDPRO/GDPC1 divergence metric does not exhibit predictive power for 60-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.02) across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: During Federal Reserve tightening cycles, a rising unemployment rate combined with stagnant payroll growth signals a labor market inflection point that historically precedes equity drawdowns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when the Fed is in a tightening phase, or predictive power vanishes in post-2010 data.
